top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The evening cycle is dominated by two emergencies and a fragile ceasefire. The WHO's Ebola public health emergency in DR Congo and Uganda keeps escalating - 131 deaths and the first American case - while the UAE is still hunting for the source of Sunday's drone strike on the Barakah nuclear plant. The Iran ceasefire holds for now, but Strait of Hormuz transit remains touchy. Markets read it all cautiously; Trump and Xi's Beijing tariff truce is still in place but increasingly threadbare.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a global health emergency
stories.summary: The WHO formally labelled the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern on May 16. The case count has now hit 131 deaths with more than 500 suspected cases, and an American national has tested positive inside the DRC.

stories.headline: UAE still investigating mystery drone strike on Barakah nuclear plant
stories.summary: Three drones crossed the western border on Sunday - two were intercepted, one ignited an electrical generator outside the inner perimeter of the Middle East's largest nuclear plant. No injuries, no radiation release, but the IAEA has voiced concern and the UAE called it a 'treacherous terrorist attack' with no group yet claiming it.

stories.headline: Israeli strike kills Hamas military wing leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad
stories.summary: Israel says it killed Haddad - described as one of the architects of the October 7, 2023 attacks - in a Gaza City strike on Friday. Six others, including his wife and daughter, were killed alongside him. The killing comes as the fragile Gaza ceasefire stalls over Hamas disarmament.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi Beijing tariff truce extended to November 10, 2026
stories.summary: Washington has extended the 10% reciprocal tariff rate for another year, keeping a fragile truce alive after this month's Beijing summit. China has paused rare earth export controls, retaliatory tariffs and Ships Fees in return - but the broader 'tariff canyon' framework remains vague on the things that actually matter.

stories.headline: Amnesty: state-sanctioned executions hit 44-year global high in 2025
stories.summary: A new Amnesty International report says the number of people killed in judicial executions worldwide reached its highest level since 1981 last year. Saudi Arabia, Iran and Iraq accounted for the majority of the surge.

stories.headline: Long Island Rail Road strike snarls New York's Monday commute
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of commuters were stranded as LIRR workers walked off the job. A tentative agreement was reached late Monday but service is still recovering, and Governor Hochul has activated emergency bus shuttles into Penn Station.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A quietly busy evening in Singapore. Thundery showers are expected most afternoons through the rest of May with peak temperatures around 33-35C, COE bidding wraps tomorrow afternoon with watchers expecting another up-move, and the CDA confirmed both MV Hondius returnees have tested negative for hantavirus. Meanwhile SM Lee Hsien Loong is on day two of his China visit. No major emergencies - but Snow City's surprise closure announcement is the day's water-cooler story.

stories.headline: CDA: both Singapore returnees from MV Hondius test negative for hantavirus
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency's National Public Health Laboratory has cleared both Singapore residents who were onboard the Andes hantavirus-hit Dutch cruise ship. They remain in quarantine for the full 30-day window with phone surveillance to 45 days as a precaution.

stories.headline: NEA: thundery showers expected most afternoons for rest of May
stories.summary: Short-duration thundery showers are forecast over parts of the island on most afternoons, sometimes extending into evening, with daily highs of 33-34C and a few days touching 35C. Pre-dawn Sumatra squalls remain a risk on some mornings.

stories.headline: COE: next bidding closes Wednesday afternoon after May 1st round set fresh highs
stories.summary: The second-round bidding exercise closes on May 20 at 4pm, after the May 1st exercise saw Category A cars at S$124,790, Cat B at S$126,236, Cat C commercial vehicles at S$87,479 and Cat E Open at S$127,700 - premiums up across every category.

stories.headline: SM Lee Hsien Loong on official visit to Guangxi and Shanghai
stories.summary: Senior Minister Lee is visiting China from May 18 to 22, holding meetings on bilateral cooperation, urban governance and the upcoming Singapore-Suzhou Industrial Park milestones.

stories.headline: Snow City to close on 30 September after 26 years
stories.summary: Singapore's first indoor snow attraction will shut at end-September, a surprise announcement that has drawn nostalgic farewells across local social feeds. Science Centre Singapore says the space will be repurposed for new climate-themed exhibits.

stories.headline: Reminder: HDB June BTO document deadline was 15 May - late applicants face delays
stories.summary: The June 2026 BTO launch covers around 6,900 flats across Ang Mo Kio, Bishan, Bukit Merah, Sembawang and Woodlands. Buyers who missed the 15 May document submission deadline have been told to wait for the October exercise.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Three theatres, three different temperatures tonight. Russia is sustaining record drone tempo against Ukraine and Kyiv has hit back deep inside Russian territory, even as Moscow publicly insists a deal is near. The Iran ceasefire is holding on paper but Sunday's Barakah drone strike has rattled the Gulf. Lebanon's truce was extended for another 45 days on May 15 yet Israeli strikes through early May still killed dozens. Nobody is fully escalating - but nobody is genuinely de-escalating either.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - record drone tempo, peace talk theatrics

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Ukraine's Kursk incursion seizes Russian border territory.
Trump administration brokers first short-lived ceasefire attempt.
Russia tests new nuclear-capable ICBM; Putin says deployment by year-end.
US-brokered three-day ceasefire collapses; Moscow resumes overnight strikes.
Russia fires 1,560+ drones at Ukraine since May 7, Zelenskyy says.
Russian strikes kill 6 in Dnipropetrovsk region.
Ukraine strikes gas facilities in Russia's Orenburg region, 1,500km from the border.
Russia Matters report: Russia lost net 12 sq mi of territory over the past week.
Ukrainian drone strike kills at least 4 near Moscow; Kremlin says peace 'progress' continues.
Putin reiterates war nearing end; Zelenskyy: 'Russia has no intention of ending this war.'

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire holding - Strait reopened, brinkmanship continues

timeline.event: US and Israel launch Operation Epic Fury - coordinated strikes on Iranian military and nuclear sites; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed.
Iran declares Strait of Hormuz 'closed'; tanker traffic drops to near zero.
US and Iran agree to ceasefire including Israel; conflict shifts to Hormuz brinkmanship.
IMO reports ~20,000 mariners and 2,000 ships stranded in Persian Gulf.
Iran's foreign minister declares Strait 'completely open' for remainder of ceasefire.
Tehran signals it will unveil a Hormuz transit toll mechanism; Trump warns clock is ticking.
Three drones strike near UAE's Barakah nuclear plant - no group claims responsibility.
Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones launched from Iraqi airspace.
UAE investigation ongoing; IAEA voices concern about nuclear-site vulnerability.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Lebanon truce extended; Gaza ceasefire fragile

timeline.event: Hamas attack on Israel triggers Gaza war.
First Israel-Lebanon ceasefire after Israel kills Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah.
Renewed war erupts in Lebanon as Israel invades again.
Israel declares 'yellow line' inside Lebanon, mirroring Gaza posture.
NPR investigation: Israel systematically destroying southern Lebanese towns and villages.
Israeli strikes in Lebanon kill 32 in a single day.
36 killed in Lebanon strikes.
51 killed in Lebanon strikes - bloodiest day of the May escalation.
Israel-Lebanon truce extended for another 45 days.
Israel kills Hamas military wing leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza City strike.
Gaza ceasefire still in place but Hamas-disarmament impasse stalls next phase.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak appears to be plateauing rather than expanding. Twelve cases (nine confirmed, two probable, one inconclusive) and three deaths with two confirmed Andes-virus aetiology - the last death was on May 2, nearly three weeks ago. The ship has docked in Rotterdam with 27 crew, former passengers are quarantined across 12 countries, and Singapore's two returnees both tested negative. WHO maintains the epidemic risk is low because spread requires close sustained contact.

stories.headline: ECDC: 12 Andes hantavirus cases, 3 deaths confirmed as MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam
stories.summary: As of May 18, twelve cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive) and three deaths are linked to the Dutch cruise ship. Two deaths are confirmed as Andes hantavirus; the last death was May 2. The vessel has now arrived in Rotterdam with 27 crew on board for disinfection and surveillance.

stories.headline: WHO: epidemic risk remains low - Andes hantavirus spreads only via close sustained contact
stories.summary: The WHO maintains a low-risk assessment for the broader population. Andes hantavirus is the only hantavirus known to spread human-to-human and may be airborne in close-contact settings, but every prior outbreak has been contained without wider community spread.

stories.headline: Former passengers hospitalised or quarantined across 12 countries
stories.summary: As of May 15, former MV Hondius passengers are under medical observation in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey and the US. Health authorities are using the 45-day maximum incubation window.

stories.headline: Singapore CDA: both local returnees test negative; quarantine continues
stories.summary: Singapore's CDA confirmed both residents who were onboard the ship tested negative across multiple samples at the National Public Health Laboratory. They remain in 30-day quarantine and 45-day phone surveillance as a precaution.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: The week's defining theme is actively-exploited zero-days outpacing patches. Cisco's SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass and Microsoft's still-unpatched Exchange zero-day are both on CISA's KEV list, cPanel's auth bypass has been exploited since February but only got a patch in late April, and BlueHammer breaks Microsoft Defender's protection. Pwn2Own Berlin paid out a record $1.3M for 47 fresh zero-days - including the first AI/LLM platform takedowns. Patch hard, patch fast.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass exploited in the wild
stories.summary: Cisco confirmed active zero-day exploitation of a critical authentication bypass that lets attackers gain administrative privileges on SD-WAN Controllers. CISA has added it to KEV with a federal patch deadline of May 17, 2026.

stories.headline: Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day - disclosed May 14, still no patch
stories.summary: A spoofing/XSS flaw affecting Exchange Server Subscription Edition, 2016 and 2019 was disclosed on May 14 with no patch available yet. Microsoft has issued mitigation guidance - restrict OWA access and force MFA - until a fix ships.

stories.headline: cPanel auth bypass - exploited since February, patched only in late April
stories.summary: Researchers describe this as one of the most actively exploited internet-facing vulnerabilities right now. cPanel disclosed and patched the auth bypass on April 28, but evidence shows ransomware crews have abused it since February - meaning many shared-hosting providers may already be compromised.

stories.headline: 'BlueHammer' Microsoft Defender flaw lets local users escalate to SYSTEM
stories.summary: BlueHammer turns Microsoft Defender - meant to be the protection layer - into a local privilege escalation vector. CISA has ordered federal agencies to patch immediately; exploitation has been observed against unmanaged endpoints.

stories.headline: Linux kernel LPE flaw added to CISA KEV - patch by May 15
stories.summary: An incorrect-resource-transfer bug in the Linux kernel allowing local privilege escalation to root has been added to CISA's KEV after confirmed in-the-wild exploitation. Fixes available in kernel 6.18.22, 6.19.12 and 7.0.

stories.headline: Android critical RCE - adjacent-attacker, no user interaction
stories.summary: Google's May Android Security Bulletin patches a critical remote code execution flaw in the Android System component that an attacker on the same Wi-Fi/Bluetooth proximity can exploit with no privileges and no user interaction.

stories.headline: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026: $1.3M paid for 47 zero-days, AI platforms fall for first time
stories.summary: DEVCORE took Master of Pwn with $505K. Windows 11, Microsoft Edge, SharePoint and VMware ESXi all fell - and crucially, the LiteLLM AI gateway and an NVVM compiler bug let researchers compromise GPU drivers and pop kernel-mode code on host machines. AI infrastructure is now a first-class target.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker zero-day with public PoC exposes protected drives
stories.summary: A BitLocker bypass with a public proof-of-concept lets attackers with brief physical access read drives encrypted under BitLocker. Microsoft is working on a fix; in the meantime, enable pre-boot PIN or password for BitLocker.
